It is classified as a woody climber, with vines that can grow up to several meters in length. The classification of Aplostylis lupuliformis is further supported by its botanical characteristics, which include its simple leaves and small white flowers. The plant's classification is crucial for understanding its evolutionary relationships with other species within the Icacinaceae family.
